pattern: Whether an element beats every element before it and every element after it can be answered in constant time per position once a running maximum from the left and a running minimum from the right are precomputed once for the whole array.
hints: Precompute, for every position, the largest value anywhere to its left and the smallest value anywhere to its right -- one left-to-right pass and one right-to-left pass.
An element scores 2 when it beats the largest value to its left and is beaten by the smallest value to its right; check that first, since it is the stronger condition.
Only if the stronger condition fails, fall back to checking the weaker, purely local condition against just its immediate neighbors for a score of 1; otherwise the position scores 0.

edge_inputs.description: smallest array where the middle element qualifies for the top score
strictly increasing, every interior position qualifies for the top score
strictly decreasing, no interior position qualifies at all
the minimum length with all identical values
values spanning from the minimum to the maximum bound while increasing
every value pinned at the maximum bound
a position that is locally increasing but not globally, so it only earns the lower score
ties (equal neighboring values) must not count toward either score

stress.description: fully random values at the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: a mostly sorted array with occasional swaps, favoring many top-score positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    nums = sorted(rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n))
    for _ in range(n // 20):
        i = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        j = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        nums[i], nums[j] = nums[j], nums[i]
    return [nums]
